chunk_summary: This protocol addresses constipation management in neurodisability, emphasizing disimpaction, maintenance, and tapering strategies tailored to dysphagia-related challenges and hydration needs.
learning_objectives: Understand the phases of constipation management: disimpaction, maintenance, and tapering in neurodisability.
Learn appropriate dosing and administration of polyethylene glycol (PEG) for disimpaction and maintenance.
Recognize neurodisability-specific considerations, including hydration strategies and anticholinergic side effects.
key_facts: Fecal impaction signs include overflow soiling, palpable fecaloma, abdominal distension, or pain.
Oral PEG disimpaction: 1.5–2 g/kg/day in two doses for 3–6 days; inpatient PEG solution: 25 mL/kg/hour for 4–6 hours.
Maintenance PEG dose: 0.5–1 g/kg/day, titrated to one soft stool daily; lactulose is an alternative.
Hydration strategies are critical due to reduced fluid intake from unsafe swallowing.
Tapering should occur after 2–3 months of success, over 2–3 months, with stool diary monitoring.
reference_quote: Suspect fecal impaction when there is overflow soiling, a palpable fecaloma, abdominal distension or pain, or long-standing constipation not responding to low-dose laxatives.
